channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, WGN 9

item.title: Fetterman claps back at Ocasio-Cortez on government funding vote
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.), who announced days ago that he would vote for a House-passed funding bill to avoid a government shutdown, said he doesn’t care about firebrand Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez’s (D-N.Y.) harsh criticism of Democratic senators who will vote to advance the controversial bill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Search goes statewide for missing Florida teen Allister Nasr
item.description: On Friday, Nasr's family was knocking on doors and handing out flyers near where police said they found Nasr's bike on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines gate agent punched in face at Dulles International Airport; passenger arrested
item.description: Police are saying that an airline passenger faces charges after he punched a United Airlines gate agent Thursday night at Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD).
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, Fox 5

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that all parents should know about
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? If so, it doesn't have to slow you down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Voters flood town halls with fears of Social Security cuts, putting heat on GOP over Musk and DOGE
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — One after another, callers on a telephone town hall with U.S. Rep. Bill Huizenga pressed the Michigan Republican about possible cuts to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Karen Read, accused killer of cop boyfriend, showed no emotion until jury deliberations: documentary
item.description: Massachusetts woman Karen Read is accused of striking her Boston Police officer boyfriend John O'Keefe with her SUV and leaving him to die in a snowstorm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Worst storms of 2025 will be outside Tornado Alley, severe weather forecast predicts
item.description: Tornadoes can (and have) hit every U.S. state, but the area nicknamed Tornado Alley is probably where your mind goes when you think of dangerous twisters. This year, that's not be the region of highest concern, according to a severe outlook released this month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KTLA 5

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tampa's Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: A collaboration decades in the making, Grammy-award winning rapper Doechii joined "Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" alums Will Smith and Tatyana Ali to recreate a viral TikTok trend.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal judge considers blocking DOGE from accessing Social Security data of millions of Americans
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A federal judge is considering whether to temporarily block Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Social Security Administration systems that hold sensitive data on millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, WGN 9

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian reporters grill Rubio over Trump's 51st state talk
item.description: Rubio said Trump’s stance on Canada joining the U.S. came following a dinner conversation with former Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Cause of death for Gene Hackman and Betsy Arakawa's dog released
item.description: An examination of the dog found dead along with actor Gene Hackman and his wife in their Santa Fe home shows dehydration and starvation were likely what led to the animal’s demise.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USPS agrees to work with DOGE on reform, planning to cut 10,000 workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Postmaster General Louis DeJoy plans to cut 10,000 workers and billions of dollars from the U.S. Postal Service budget and he’ll do that working with Elon Musk ’s Department of Government Efficiency, according to a letter sent to members of Congress on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Popular coffee creamers sold in 31 states are being recalled, according to the Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Congress poised to force $1B cut to local DC budget, surprising many lawmakers
item.description: Congress is poised to pass a President Trump-endorsed funding bill this week that Washington, D.C., officials warn would lead to a $1 billion cut to the District’s local budget, a move that has been catching members of both parties off guard.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Infant safe; 2 arrested as SFPD looking for 3rd suspect
item.description: UPDATED 2 p.m.: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (KELO) — The child that was kidnapped Thursday evening in Sioux Falls was found unharmed, police said in a news release shortly before 2 p.m. Friday. The Sioux Falls Police Department announced officers arrested two people in connection with the kidnapping.
item.dc:creator: Collete Stellinga, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin authorities release details on missing teen's recovery
item.description: Lillian Asala, a 13-year-old from Wisconsin, was reported missing on March 1. Her mother was arrested in connection to her disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, NewsNation

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Second judge blocks mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: A second federal judge has blocked the Trump administration from firing federal workers, issuing an order Thursday that probationary employees be reinstated weeks after their dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Spring break travelers urged to take certain precautions amid search for missing American college student
item.description: Former U.S. ambassador to the Dominican Republic Robin Bernstein, who served between 2018 and 2021, has a warning for spring breakers in light of the disappearance of Sudiksha Konanki.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbine victim's 2025 death ruled a homicide after injuries from school shooting contributed to sepsis
item.description: The February 2025 death of Anne Marie Hochhalter, who was partially paralyzed when she was shot during the Columbine High School massacre, has been ruled a homicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Khalil protestors fill up Trump Tower
item.description: Protesters opposed to the detention of Palestinian activist Mahmoud Khalil filled up Trump Tower in New York on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KRON4

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
